Details: Although GAC adsorption was found to be relatively effective for the removal of 1,4-dioxane at the bench scale (67 to 87 percent) [2067, 2731]., pilot- and full-scale studies showed much lower effectiveness. One pilot-scale study reported 18 percent removal of 1,4-dioxane using a peat-based GAC material [2110]. The study also found that 56 percent of the contaminant was removed using GAC downstream of reverse osmosis. Two full-scale studies displayed poor removal of 1,4-dioxane, achieving a range of less than 0 to 27 percent [2722, 2732]. These low percent removals are indicative of early 1,4-dioxane breakthrough through the GAC..
